title: potentilla aurea var. latiloba
intro: Potentilla aurea var. latiloba, also known as the golden potentilla or five-petaled cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) with bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Potentilla aurea var. latiloba include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
